content:
Imagine the stock market like a big playground where people buy and sell pieces of companies, called stocks. Sometimes, lots of people want to buy a stock because they believe the company will do really well, like winning a game. When more people want to buy than sell, the price goes up! But other times, people get worried the company might not do well, like losing a game, so they sell their stocks. When more people want to sell than buy, the price goes down.

Also, news can change how people feel. If a new toy from a company is super popular, its stock might go up. If there’s a problem, like a broken toy, the stock might go down. So, the stock market moves up and down because people’s feelings, like hope or worry, and what they think will happen next, keep changing. It’s like a big game where everyone’s guesses make the prices jump up and down!
